title: Congress Introduces DHS Use of Force Transparency Act Amid ICE Shooting Deaths
excerpt: Representatives Dave Min and Ayanna Pressley introduced the DHS Use of Force Transparency Act of 2026, requiring DHS to turn over all evidence related to ICE-caused injuries and deaths to Congress, including body camera footage and incident reports. The bill was introduced in direct response to the deaths of at least three Americans at the hands of ICE and CBP agents.
content:
On March 25, 2026, Representatives Dave Min (CA-47) and Ayanna Pressley (MA-07) introduced the DHS Use of Force Transparency Act of 2026 (H.R. 7984), seeking to compel greater accountability over ICE use-of-force incidents.

  • The legislation would require DHS to turn over all evidence related to ICE-caused injuries and deaths to Congress, including body camera footage and incident reports.
  • The bill followed reports that at least three U.S. citizens had been killed by ICE and CBP agents, and that Congress was being denied access to critical evidence.
  • Rep. Min stated: "Congress must assert its oversight authority to investigate these abuses and protect Americans from aggressive, inadequately trained officers."
  • The bill was referred to the House Committees on Homeland Security, the Judiciary, and Ways and Means.

The introduction of the bill marked a congressional effort to institutionalize oversight mechanisms that the executive branch had been resisting since the start of Operation Metro Surge.
source: U.S. House of Representatives / Rep. Dave Min Press Release
